Chapter: Church Organization Chart.
Current document status: Needs Bylaw Review.

Section: KBC Organization Chart.

Section: Purpose.

This document provides a working organization chart for Kingsville Baptist Church and shows how financial governance, ministry leadership, committees, elected officers, and operational support relate to one another.

It is intended to help leaders see the difference between:

Congregational authority.
Elected officers and committees.
Ministry leadership.
Financial operations support.
Committee recommendations and coordination.

Needs Bylaw Review: This chart should be checked against the KBC Constitution and Bylaws, committee charters, and current church practice before being treated as authoritative.

Note: Congregation Final Authority.
The congregation remains the final authority where KBC bylaws, budget, policy, officer election, major non-budgeted spending, or church practice require a vote.

Section: Simple Governance View.

Diagram omitted. See the handbook site for the visual version.

Section: Financial Operations View.

The detailed financial reporting and coordination diagram now has its own navigation entry:

Open the Financial Operations View.

Section: Practical Role Summary.

Table summary.
Body / Role: Congregation. Primary Accountability: Final church authority where bylaws, budget, policy, officer election, or church practice require a vote.. Ongoing Role: Approves the annual budget and other matters reserved for congregational action; receives clear reports and recommendations..
Body / Role: Pastor. Primary Accountability: Spiritual and ministry leadership, communication, and counsel.. Ongoing Role: Helps church bodies coordinate their work while preserving the authority assigned to each body..
Body / Role: Deacons. Primary Accountability: Servant leadership, care, counsel, and leadership review where church practice requires.. Ongoing Role: May raise concerns, offer counsel, and recommend matters to the appropriate committee or congregation without becoming a unilateral financial authority..
Body / Role: Nominating Committee. Primary Accountability: Officer and committee nomination process where assigned by bylaws.. Ongoing Role: Recommends officers and committee members through the process assigned by the bylaws and church practice..
Body / Role: Finance Committee. Primary Accountability: Financial governance, controls, budget oversight, software, monthly review, and financial policies.. Ongoing Role: Defines financial controls, recommends policies and budgets, reviews financial activity, and brings matters to the congregation where approval is required..
Body / Role: Personnel Committee. Primary Accountability: Job descriptions, hiring, compensation recommendations, and evaluation process.. Ongoing Role: Manages personnel processes while receiving operational requirements from the committee responsible for the work..
Body / Role: Treasurer / Financial Officer. Primary Accountability: Elected financial accountability, reporting, review, and coordination with Finance Committee.. Ongoing Role: Reviews reports, helps present financial information, helps carry out approved Finance Committee direction, confirms policies are followed, and coordinates with the Bookkeeper and Finance Committee without becoming the sole point of financial control..
Body / Role: Bookkeeper / Financial Administrator. Primary Accountability: Day-to-day financial operations under assigned supervision and approved financial controls.. Ongoing Role: Maintains records, enters transactions, supports deposits, processes reimbursements after approval, prepares report packets, and supports monthly close..
Body / Role: Outside Payroll / Accounting Vendor. Primary Accountability: Professional processing and compliance support if contracted.. Ongoing Role: Handles payroll, tax forms, accounting setup, review, or other specialized services assigned by church-approved process..

Section: Recommended Reporting And Coordination Lines.

These lines should be confirmed through the Constitution and Bylaws, committee charters, adopted policies, and church practice:
